rsmTopic: Set theory: defined sets, equal sets, empty sets
description: Understanding sets and their properties - RSM introduces this in grade 3, well above US standards
notes: Set theory is foundational for logic and later algebra. US Common Core introduces this implicitly in grade 4-5.

description: Using Venn diagrams to show relationships between sets
notes: Visual logic tool. Bridges set theory to later probability and statistics.

description: Multiplying numbers like 234 x 6 using standard algorithm
notes: CCSS places 4.NBT.B.5 at grade 4. RSM does this in grade 3.

rsmTopic: Multiplication and division by powers of 10
description: Understanding how multiplying/dividing by 10, 100, 1000 shifts digits
notes: Connects to place value understanding. CCSS 4.NBT.A.1.

rsmTopic: Units of measurement: length, weight, time
description: Converting between measurement units and solving measurement word problems
notes: CCSS 4.MD.A.1. RSM includes more complex conversions.

description: Multiplying numbers like 34 x 56 or 123 x 45
notes: CCSS 4.NBT.B.5 covers 2-digit multiplication. RSM extends to 3-digit in grade 3.

description: Line symmetry, reflection, translation of shapes on a plane
notes: CCSS addresses this in grade 4 (4.G.A.3) and grade 5 geometry.

description: Using letters to represent unknown quantities in simple equations
notes: CCSS introduces expressions in grade 5 (5.OA.A). RSM does this in grade 3 Q2.

description: Solving word problems using the distance formula d = v × t
notes: This is formally a middle school topic (grade 6-7 in US). RSM covers it in grade 3. Requires multi-digit multiplication.

description: Applying multiplication to real-world cost calculations
notes: Practical application of multiplication. Builds formula-thinking habits.

description: Solving problems about work done at a given rate over time
notes: This is typically a grade 6-7 concept in US curriculum. RSM introduces it in grade 3.

description: Solving word problems that require 3 or more operations
notes: CCSS grade 3 covers 2-step problems. RSM grade 3 Q4 covers compound multi-step problems.

description: Finding equivalent fractions and comparing fractions with different denominators
notes: Aligns well with CCSS grade 4 (4.NF.A.1, 4.NF.A.2).

description: Adding and subtracting fractions with like and unlike denominators
notes: CCSS places unlike denominators at grade 5 (5.NF.A.1). RSM covers at grade 4.

description: Reading, writing, and comparing decimal numbers
notes: Aligns with CCSS grade 4 (4.NF.C.6, 4.NF.C.7).

rsmTopic: Area and perimeter of complex figures
description: Computing area and perimeter of L-shapes and composite figures
notes: CCSS covers this at grade 5-6.

description: Plotting points using (x, y) coordinates in the first quadrant
notes: CCSS grade 5 (5.G.A.1).

description: Solving equations of the form x + 5 = 12 or 3x = 18
notes: Extends the variable intro from grade 3.

description: Multiplying and dividing fractions and mixed numbers
notes: CCSS grade 5 (5.NF.B.4, 5.NF.B.7).

description: Understanding ratio notation and solving proportions
notes: CCSS grade 6 (6.RP.A.1-3). RSM introduces at grade 5.

description: Converting between fractions, decimals, and percentages. Percent of a number.
notes: CCSS addresses formally at grade 6. RSM covers at grade 5.

description: Number line with negative numbers, ordering, and absolute value
notes: CCSS grade 6 (6.NS.C.5-7).

description: Solving one-variable linear equations with variables on both sides
notes: CCSS grade 7 (7.EE.B.4). RSM covers at grade 5.

description: Computing and interpreting measures of central tendency
notes: CCSS grade 6 (6.SP.B.5).

description: Systematic counting, fundamental counting principle, tree diagrams
notes: Combinatorics is a RSM specialty. Introduced informally in grade 4, more formally in grade 5-6.

description: Solving systems of two linear equations using substitution and graphing
notes: CCSS grade 8 (8.EE.C.8). RSM introduces at grade 6.

description: Understanding the concept of a function, domain, range, and graphing linear functions
notes: CCSS grade 8 (8.F.A). RSM introduces at grade 6.

description: Solving linear inequalities and representing solutions on a number line
notes: CCSS grade 7 (7.EE.B.4b). RSM formalizes at grade 6.

description: Integer exponents, product rule, power rule, and scientific notation
notes: CCSS grade 8 (8.EE.A.1-4). RSM covers at grade 6.

description: Understanding and applying the Pythagorean theorem to find side lengths and distances
notes: CCSS grade 8 (8.G.B.7-8). RSM introduces at grade 6.

description: Computing theoretical probability, running experiments, comparing results
notes: CCSS grade 7 (7.SP.C.5-7). RSM covers at grade 6.

description: Adding, subtracting, and multiplying polynomials (intro level)
notes: High school Algebra 1 (CCSS HSA-APR.A.1). RSM introduces at grade 6.

rsmTopic: Factoring: GCF and difference of squares
description: Factoring polynomials using greatest common factor and difference of squares pattern
notes: CCSS Algebra 1 (HSA-SSE.A.2). RSM introduces at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Quadratic equations: factoring and quadratic formula
description: Solving quadratic equations by factoring and using the quadratic formula
notes: CCSS Algebra 1 (HSA-REI.B.4). RSM introduces at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Proportional geometry: similar triangles and scale factors
description: Identifying similar triangles, finding scale factors, solving for unknown side lengths
notes: CCSS grade 8 (8.G.A.4-5). RSM formalizes at grade 7.

description: Introduction to logical arguments, if-then statements, and two-column geometric proofs
notes: Geometry proofs are CCSS grade 9-10. RSM introduces deductive reasoning at grade 7.

description: Factorial notation, nPr, nCr, and applying counting methods to probability problems
notes: CCSS Statistics in Algebra 2 / grade 11. RSM covers at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Systems of linear equations: substitution and elimination
description: Solving systems of two linear equations using substitution and elimination methods
notes: CCSS introduces systems at grade 8. RSM covers at grade 6 with full algebraic approach.

rsmTopic: Inequalities and their graphs on a number line
description: Understanding and solving linear inequalities, representing solutions on number lines
notes: CCSS grade 7 algebra standard. RSM treats these earlier with more rigor.

rsmTopic: Number theory: prime factorization, GCF, LCM
description: Finding prime factorizations, computing GCF and LCM using prime factorization
notes: Aligned with CCSS grade 6 NS standards, but RSM emphasizes Euclidean algorithm approach.

rsmTopic: Geometric proofs: congruence and similarity using axioms
description: Formal two-column proofs for triangle congruence (SSS, SAS, ASA) and similarity
notes: CCSS Geometry standard (grade 10). RSM introduces formal proof at grade 6.

description: Setting up and solving proportions; calculating percent increase and decrease
notes: CCSS grade 7 RP standard. RSM emphasizes cross-multiplication and algebraic setups.

description: Properties of integer exponents (including negative exponents), converting to/from scientific notation
notes: CCSS grade 8 EE standard. RSM covers at grade 6 to prepare for algebra.

rsmTopic: Coordinate geometry: distance and midpoint formulas
description: Computing distances between points and finding midpoints using coordinate methods
notes: CCSS grade 8 / Geometry standard. RSM introduces at grade 6 alongside algebra.

rsmTopic: Combinatorics: counting principle and tree diagrams
description: Fundamental counting principle, tree diagrams, and sample spaces for probability
notes: CCSS grade 7 Statistics. RSM emphasizes systematic counting methods.

description: Finding nth terms, sums, and identifying patterns in arithmetic and geometric sequences
notes: CCSS Algebra 1 standard. RSM covers at grade 7 to build algebraic fluency.

description: Adding, subtracting, and multiplying polynomials including FOIL and special products
notes: CCSS Algebra 1 / grade 9 standard. RSM introduces polynomial algebra at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Factoring polynomials: GCF, trinomials, difference of squares
description: Factoring techniques: pulling out GCF, factoring ax²+bx+c trinomials, recognizing special forms
notes: CCSS Algebra 1 standard. RSM covers complete factoring at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Functions: definition, domain, range, and notation
description: Understanding functions as mappings, notation f(x), vertical line test, domain and range
notes: CCSS grade 8 F standards. RSM introduces function concept formally at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Quadratic equations: solving by factoring and quadratic formula
description: Solving ax²+bx+c=0 by factoring, completing the square, and the quadratic formula
notes: CCSS Algebra 1 standard. RSM covers all quadratic methods at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Probability: theoretical vs. experimental, conditional probability
description: Computing theoretical probabilities, comparing to experimental results, introducing P(A|B)
notes: CCSS grade 7-8 Statistics. RSM adds conditional probability context at grade 7.

rsmTopic: Number theory: divisibility rules and modular arithmetic
description: Divisibility tests for 2-12, clock arithmetic, and basic modular congruences
notes: Not in CCSS. RSM covers number theory systematically as enrichment/competition prep.

rsmTopic: Word problems involving rates, mixtures, and work
description: Setting up and solving systems of equations for classic rate, distance, mixture, and work problems
notes: Applied algebra problem types emphasized in RSM and competition math at grade 7.
